2605.20172.title: Long-term Power Grid Planning via Answer Set Programming
2605.20172.paper_summary: The Power grid is a critical infrastructure underpinning all aspects of modern society and its services. Maintaining its effectiveness requires continuous adaptations. In particular, addressing sustainability targets, demand patterns, and urbanisation trends requires implementing changes to the network. Actual developments can potentially span over a decade, with supply continuity and service quality that must be preserved throughout by ensuring conformance to several topological and combinatorial invariants. Long-term power grid planning deals with the above process, and although planning languages could be a natural choice, the kind of properties and invariants needed are cumbersome to express in such languages; on the contrary, they can be elegantly and succinctly encoded in Answer Set Programming (ASP). In this paper, we propose the first approach to automate and optimise the long-term power grid planning process using ASP. Experimental evaluations conducted on synthetic and real-world grid data confirm the expressive power of the proposed ASP-based approach and demonstrate its effectiveness.
2605.20172.paper_summary_zh: 電力網是支撐現代社會及其服務各個方面的關鍵基礎設施。<lb/>維持其有效性需要持續的適應。<lb/>特別是，應對可持續性目標、需求模式和城市化趨勢需要對網絡進行變更。<lb/>實際的發展可能會跨越十年，供應連續性和服務質量必須在整個過程中保持，這需要確保遵守多個拓撲和組合不變量。<lb/>長期電力網規劃處理上述過程，儘管規劃語言可能是自然的選擇，但所需的屬性和不變量在這些語言中表達起來相當繁瑣；相反，它們可以在答案集編程（ASP）中優雅且簡潔地編碼。<lb/>在本文中，我們提出了第一種利用ASP自動化和優化長期電力網規劃過程的方法。<lb/>在合成和真實世界電網數據上進行的實驗評估確認了所提議的基於ASP的方法的表達能力，並展示了其有效性。
2605.20172.author: Antonio Ielo et.al.
2605.20172.authors: Antonio Ielo, Francesco Doria, Sandra Castellanos-Paez, Marco Maratea, Francesco Percassi, Mauro Vallati
